7	# Feature: DDx Website
8	
9	**ID:** FEAT-003
10	**Status:** In Progress
11	**Priority:** P0
12	**Owner:** DDx Team
13	
14	## Overview
15	
16	`ddx.github.io` is the promotional and documentation website for DDx. It explains what document-driven development is, why DDx exists, how to get started, and how the ecosystem fits together. Hosted on GitHub Pages.
17	
18	## Problem Statement
19	
20	**Current situation:** DDx has a README but no dedicated website. New visitors must parse a GitHub repo to understand what DDx is and whether it's useful to them.
21	
22	**Pain points:**
23	- No landing page that explains the concept to someone who hasn't seen it before
24	- No visual explanation of the ecosystem (DDx, workflow tools, agents)
25	- Installation and quick-start instructions buried in a README
26	- No SEO presence — developers searching for document management tooling won't find DDx
27	
28	**Desired outcome:** A clean, fast website that explains DDx in under 2 minutes, gets a developer to installation in under 5 minutes, and serves as the canonical reference for the project.
29	
30	## Requirements
31	
32	### Functional
33	
34	1. **Landing page** — hero section explaining DDx in one sentence, visual ecosystem diagram, call-to-action to install
35	2. **Quick start** — copy-pasteable installation command, 3-step getting started guide
36	3. **Concepts page** — explains document-driven development, document types, how DDx fits with agents and workflow tools
37	4. **CLI reference** — command documentation (can be generated from CLI help text)
38	5. **Ecosystem page** — shows DDx's position as infrastructure, lists workflow tools built on DDx, highlights HELIX as first plugin
39	6. **Server documentation** — how to run ddx-server, MCP endpoint reference
40	7. **Embedded terminal demos** — asciinema recordings of core workflows embedded in hero section and getting-started page:
41	   - Install DDx
42	   - `ddx init` + `ddx list` + `ddx doctor`
43	   - `ddx install helix` (plugin bootstrap)
44	   - One-shot project creation with HELIX
45	   - Feature evolution with HELIX
46	8. **README** — animated GIF/SVG demos, badge row, plugin quick start, link to microsite. The README is the GitHub-facing landing page and must sell at a glance.
47	
48	### Non-Functional
49	
50	- **Performance:** Static site, loads in <2 seconds on 3G
51	- **Hosting:** GitHub Pages, no server infrastructure needed
52	- **Maintenance:** Content derived from repo docs where possible, minimal manual upkeep
53	- **Design:** Clean, developer-focused. No enterprise fluff. Code examples prominent.
54	- **SEO:** Targets "document-driven development", "AI agent document management", "MCP document server"
55	
56	## User Stories
57	
58	### US-020: Developer Discovers DDx
59	**As a** developer searching for tools to manage AI agent documents
60	**I want** to land on a page that explains what DDx does in 30 seconds
61	**So that** I can decide whether to try it
62	
63	**Acceptance Criteria:**
64	- Given I visit ddx.github.io, when the page loads, then I see a clear one-sentence description and a visual diagram
65	- Given I'm interested, when I scroll down, then I see a quick-start section with a copy-pasteable install command
66	- Given I want details, when I click "Learn more", then I reach a concepts page explaining document-driven development
67	
68	### US-021: Developer Installs DDx from Website
69	**As a** developer who decided to try DDx
70	**I want** to copy an install command and get started
71	**So that** I go from website to working tool in under 5 minutes
72	
73	**Acceptance Criteria:**
74	- Given I'm on the quick-start section, when I click the install command, then it copies to my clipboard
75	- Given I've installed DDx, when I follow the "First steps" guide on the site, then I have a working document library in my project
76	
77	### US-023: Developer Sees DDx in Action Before Installing
78	**As a** developer evaluating DDx
79	**I want** to watch a terminal recording of the core workflow
80	**So that** I can see what DDx actually does before committing to install it
81	
82	**Acceptance Criteria:**
83	- Given I visit the landing page, when it loads, then I see an embedded terminal recording in or near the hero section
84	- Given I visit the getting-started page, then each step has a corresponding recording I can watch
85	- Given I visit the ecosystem page, then I see a recording of `ddx install helix` bootstrapping the workflow plugin
86	
87	### US-024: Developer Evaluates DDx from GitHub README
88	**As a** developer who finds DDx on GitHub
89	**I want** the README to show me what DDx does in under 30 seconds
90	**So that** I decide whether to click through to the full site
91	
92	**Acceptance Criteria:**
93	- Given I open the GitHub repo, when the README renders, then I see an animated terminal demo (GIF or SVG) of the init workflow
94	- Given I scroll the README, then I see a plugin install example and a link to the microsite
95	
96	### US-022: Developer Understands the Ecosystem
97	**As a** developer evaluating DDx
98	**I want** to understand how DDx relates to workflow tools and agents
99	**So that** I know what DDx does vs what I need to get elsewhere
100	
101	**Acceptance Criteria:**
102	- Given I visit the ecosystem page, when I read it, then I understand that DDx is infrastructure and workflow tools are separate
103	- Given I see the diagram, then I can identify what layer DDx occupies vs HELIX vs agents
104	
105	## Edge Cases
106	
107	- Visitor on mobile — site must be responsive
108	- Visitor with JavaScript disabled — core content must render without JS (demo recordings degrade to static screenshots or text)
109	- Stale documentation — establish process for regenerating CLI docs from source
110	- Stale demo recordings — CI regenerates recordings when CLI changes; PR opened if recordings differ
111	
112	## Implementation
113	
114	- **Static site generator:** Hugo with Hextra theme (Tailwind CSS, hero shortcodes, dark mode, search)
115	- **Location:** `/website/` directory in monorepo
116	- **Deployment:** GitHub Actions → GitHub Pages (`.github/workflows/pages.yml`)
117	- **Hugo module:** `github.com/imfing/hextra`
118	
119	### Pages Built
120	
121	- Landing page with hero, feature grid, CTAs
122	- Getting Started (install, init, first steps)
123	- Concepts (document-driven development explanation)
124	- CLI Reference (all commands)
125	- DDx Server (MCP endpoints, HTTP API — marked as under development)
126	- Ecosystem (stack diagram, what-goes-where table)
127	
128	### Demo Recording Pipeline
129	
130	- Reproducible scripts in `scripts/demos/` drive asciinema recordings
131	- Each script produces a `.cast` file rendered to GIF/SVG via agg or svg-term
132	- Rendered assets live in `website/static/demos/` for Hugo embedding
133	- Hugo shortcode or partial wraps asciinema-player for `.cast` playback
134	- CI workflow (`.github/workflows/demos.yml`) regenerates on CLI changes
135	
136	## Dependencies
137	
138	- Hugo 0.159+ extended
139	- Hextra theme via Hugo modules
140	- GitHub Pages hosting
141	- Go toolchain (for Hugo modules)
142	- asciinema (recording) + agg or svg-term (rendering) for demo pipeline
143	- CI workflow for demo regeneration (`.github/workflows/demos.yml`)
144	- CI gate: pages deployment depends on CI passing
145	
146	## Out of Scope
147	
148	- Interactive document browser (P1 — would integrate with ddx-server)
149	- User accounts or login
150	- Blog or news feed
151	- Community forum (use GitHub Discussions)
152	- Localization / i18n
153

/tmp/ddx-redesign-plan-2026-05-01.md
# DDx Site + Spec Redesign Plan — 2026-05-01

## Context

The DDx marketing site has drifted from current spec state and uses inflated principle copy. We're realigning around (a) a software-factory framing, (b) a two-layer principle structure (10 domain-facing, 6 engineering-internal), and (c) HELIX-aligned research/reference artifacts that back each principle. This plan was developed via multi-round dialogue and codex review across two sessions.

## Locked decisions (from prior dialogue)

### Section D answers
- `run` / `try` / `work` is the new unified naming; retire `execute-loop` / `execute-bead` as primary names
- Artifacts and the artifact graph are the spine of DDx (it's in the name)
- Drop **Dun** entirely from copy (not a peer, may never happen)
- Quorum review is just a skill — do not promote to primary capability

### Software factory framing
- Add lineage section to `docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md`
- Add preface paragraph to `docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md` framing principles as factory-design choices
- Create `website/content/docs/concepts/software-factory.md`
- Homepage gains sub-claim "A document-driven software factory."

### Resource consolidation into HELIX 00-discover
- Research synthesis lives in `docs/helix/00-discover/research/<slug>.md` with `RSCH-NNN` IDs
- External references live in `docs/helix/00-discover/references/<slug>.md` with `REF-NNN` IDs
- Migrate `docs/resources/` into the right 00-discover bucket; deprecate the old folder (stub README pointing forward)
- Register `research` and `reference` artifact kinds in `FEAT-005`
- Principle list cites research artifacts via `depends_on`

### Two-layer principle split
- **Domain principles** (user-facing) live on `/why` and `/docs/principles/<slug>/` and in `docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md`
- **Engineering principles** (internal) live in `CONTRIBUTING.md` (or `docs/dev/engineering-principles.md`)

### Domain principles (10, locked)

1. **Spec-first development.**
2. **Executable specifications.**
3. **Audit trail required.**
4. **Context is king.**
5. **Work is a DAG.**
6. **Right-size the model.**
7. **Avoid vendor lock-in.**
8. **Drift is debt.**
9. **Least privilege for agents.**
10. **Inspect and adapt.**

### Engineering principles (6, locked)

1. **Platform, Not Methodology** — DDx ships primitives; workflow tools ship opinions.
2. **Project-Local by Default** — install and config write only under `<projectRoot>/`.
3. **Bounded Context per Attempt** — every attempt runs in a fresh, narrow context against an explicit contract.
4. **Evidence on Disk** — any state that must survive a single attempt is a file in the repo.
5. **Cheap-Default, Escalate on Failure** — routing picks the cheapest competent tier first.
6. **Reversible Over Ergonomic** — when two designs are roughly equivalent, prefer the one easier to undo.

## Workstreams

### W1 — Software factory framing
**Goal**: Position DDx as a document-driven software factory across vision, principles, and site.

W1.1: Add "Lineage" section to `docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md` referencing software-factory tradition (Greenfield/Short Microsoft 2007), spec-driven movement (spec-kit, Kiro), and current agent-execution wave. Cross-link to `REF-*` artifacts.

W1.2: Add preface paragraph to `docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md` framing the principles as "factory-floor design choices given that DDx is a document-driven software factory." This preface frames whichever principle list ends up there (see W3).

W1.3: Create `website/content/docs/concepts/software-factory.md` — concept page explaining the metaphor, what DDx inherits, what it adds, what it intentionally does not do.

W1.4: Update `website/content/_index.md` (homepage) — add sub-claim under existing hero. Add link to new `/docs/concepts/software-factory/` page.

### W2 — HELIX-aligned research/reference migration
**Goal**: All prior-art and research artifacts live in HELIX 00-discover with stable IDs and structured frontmatter.

W2.1: Update `FEAT-005` (Artifact Convention) to register two new artifact kinds:
  - `research` — first-party research synthesis (lives in `docs/helix/00-discover/research/`, ID prefix `RSCH-NNN`, frontmatter: `id, title, summary, depends_on, accessed`)
  - `reference` — external reference material (lives in `docs/helix/00-discover/references/`, ID prefix `REF-NNN`, frontmatter: `id, title, kind, source_url, source_author, accessed, summary, tags`)

W2.2: Migrate existing `docs/resources/` files into 00-discover with `REF-NNN` IDs and normalized frontmatter:
  - `spec-driven.md` → `REF-001` (references/spec-driven.md)
  - `ai-agent-frameworks-2025.md` → `REF-002`
  - `microsoft-ai-agents-2025.md` → `REF-003`
  - `model-catalog.md` → `REF-004`
  - `agent-harness-ac.md` → `REF-005`
  - `agent-harness-ac-work.md` → `REF-006`

W2.3: Add new external references (`REF-NNN`):
  - GitHub Spec Kit (`spec-kit` repo)
  - AWS Kiro (`kiro.dev`)
  - Greenfield/Short, *Software Factories* (Microsoft, 2004)
  - Liu et al, *Lost in the Middle* (TACL 2024)
  - Chroma 2025 *Context Rot* study
  - Sheridan & Verplank 1978 *Levels of Automation*
  - OWASP LLM Top 10 (2025)
  - MAST 2025 (Multi-Agent Failure Taxonomy)
  - Pat Helland, *Data on the Outside vs the Inside* (ACM Queue)
  - FrugalGPT
  - Self-Refine (NeurIPS 2023)
  - Knuth, *Literate Programming*

W2.4: Stub `docs/resources/README.md` pointing at `docs/helix/00-discover/`; remove all migrated files from `docs/resources/`.

### W3 — Domain principles: research artifacts, deep pages, /why rewrite
**Goal**: Each of the 10 domain principles has a research artifact (RSCH-NNN), a deep site page, and a summary on /why.

W3.1: Create 10 research synthesis artifacts at `docs/helix/00-discover/research/<slug>.md` (`RSCH-001` through `RSCH-010`). Each cites the relevant `REF-*` artifacts via `depends_on` and contains 300–500 words of synthesis with claims, evidence, and DDx implications.

| ID | Slug | Principle | Cites |
|---|---|---|---|
| RSCH-001 | spec-first-development | #1 | spec-kit, Kiro, REF-005 (HumanEval) |
| RSCH-002 | executable-specifications | #2 | EvalPlus, BDD lineage |
| RSCH-003 | audit-trail-required | #3 | Helland, Kleppmann, Fowler |
| RSCH-004 | context-is-king | #4 | Lost in the Middle, Chroma context rot, RAG canon |
| RSCH-005 | work-is-a-dag | #5 | CPM/PERT, Bazel/Dask docs |
| RSCH-006 | right-size-the-model | #6 | FrugalGPT, multi-model routing 2026 |
| RSCH-007 | avoid-vendor-lock-in | #7 | LSP m+n, POSIX/SQL/OCI lineage |
| RSCH-008 | drift-is-debt | #8 | Knuth literate programming, doc-drift studies |
| RSCH-009 | least-privilege-for-agents | #9 | Saltzer & Schroeder 1975, OWASP LLM01, EchoLeak CVE |
| RSCH-010 | inspect-and-adapt | #10 | Sheridan & Verplank, MAST 2025, Self-Refine |

W3.2: Update `docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md` — replace existing content with the 10 domain principles + the W1.2 preface; each principle's `depends_on` lists its `RSCH-NNN`.

W3.3: Create 10 deep pages at `website/content/docs/principles/<slug>/index.md`. Each page rendered from the corresponding `RSCH-NNN` content, with claim → evidence → DDx-feature-shaped sections.

W3.4: Rewrite `website/content/why/_index.md` — one section per principle, 50–80 word summary linking to the `/docs/principles/<slug>/` deep page.

### W4 — Engineering principles internal doc
**Goal**: Six engineering principles documented as the internal contributor reference.

W4.1: Create `docs/dev/engineering-principles.md` (or extend `CONTRIBUTING.md`) with the 6 engineering principles, each with: rule, decision generated, alternative rejected, tradeoff, real DDx feature it shapes.

W4.2: Cross-reference from `docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md` preface (which now houses domain principles): "For DDx-internal architecture decisions, see `docs/dev/engineering-principles.md`."

### W5 — IA changes (existing site drift fixes)

W5.1: Three-layer run model concept page — create `website/content/docs/concepts/run-architecture.md` describing `ddx run` / `ddx try` / `ddx work` as one architecture. Update `concepts/architecture` and homepage to use this naming. Retire `execute-loop` / `execute-bead` from primary copy (keep in CLI reference).

W5.2: Skill consolidation — collapse the 6 site skill pages (`/ddx-bead`, `/ddx-agent`, `/ddx-install`, `/ddx-status`, `/ddx-review`, `/ddx-run`) into a single `ddx` skill page. Gated on `FEAT-011` landing; if FEAT-011 ships during this redesign, do this; otherwise file as a follow-up bead with explicit dep on FEAT-011.

W5.3: CLI docs slimming — replace auto-generated `cli/commands/` (130+ files) page with a curated noun-verb reference matching `CLAUDE.md`'s command overview. Keep auto-generated full ref behind a "complete reference" link if desired.

W5.4: Surface missing capabilities — add `ddx sync` (FEAT-023) to the relevant concept page; add multi-node dashboard (FEAT-020/021) to the server section.

W5.5: Maturity honesty pass — re-badge "multi-model review (quorum)" from beta to **framing** (matches FEAT-013 status); re-badge plugin API from stable to **not-started** (matches FEAT-018); review server UI screenshots and replace placeholders with current state or remove.

W5.6: Drop Dun from copy — search and remove all references to Dun in the site content (`/docs/concepts`, ecosystem page, glossary).

W5.7: De-emphasize quorum review — remove from features-list prominence; mention only in the skills documentation.

### W6 — Glue and verification

W6.1: Build the site locally (`cd website && hugo`) and verify all internal links resolve. Specifically check the `/why` → `/docs/principles/<slug>/` cross-links and `RSCH-NNN` ↔ `REF-NNN` cross-references.

W6.2: Update `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-003-website.md` to reflect new site IA.

W6.3: Run `ddx doc audit` (artifact graph) to confirm all new artifacts (`RSCH-*`, `REF-*`) are properly indexed and no staleness warnings fire on the new principles.

## Bead breakdown

A parent epic (`epic`) with child beads grouped by workstream. Dependencies enforce order: FEAT-005 update before any RSCH/REF creation; W2 before W3 (because RSCH cites REF); W3 before W4 (engineering principles preface references domain principle home).

| Bead ID (placeholder) | Title | Type | Depends on | Workstream |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| EPIC-A | Site + spec redesign 2026-05 | epic | — | — |
| B1 | Update FEAT-005 to register research and reference artifact kinds | task | EPIC-A | W2 |
| B2 | Migrate docs/resources/ → HELIX 00-discover/references/ with REF-NNN | task | B1 | W2 |
| B3 | Author new REF-NNN artifacts (spec-kit, Kiro, MS factories, etc.) | task | B1 | W2 |
| B4 | Author 10 RSCH-NNN research synthesis artifacts | task | B2, B3 | W3 |
| B5 | Update docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md with new 10 + preface + depends_on | task | B4 | W3 |
| B6 | Create 10 /docs/principles/<slug>/ deep pages on website | task | B4 | W3 |
| B7 | Rewrite website/content/why/_index.md with 10 sections + cross-links | task | B6 | W3 |
| B8 | Add Lineage section to product-vision.md cross-linking REF-* | task | B3 | W1 |
| B9 | Add software-factory framing preface to principles.md | task | B5 | W1 |
| B10 | Create website/content/docs/concepts/software-factory.md | task | B3 | W1 |
| B11 | Update homepage with software-factory sub-claim and link | task | B10 | W1 |
| B12 | Create docs/dev/engineering-principles.md | task | EPIC-A | W4 |
| B13 | Cross-reference engineering vs domain principle docs | task | B5, B12 | W4 |
| B14 | Three-layer run-architecture concept page; retire execute-loop naming | task | EPIC-A | W5 |
| B15 | Skill consolidation on site (gated on FEAT-011 status) | task | EPIC-A | W5 |
| B16 | Curated noun-verb CLI reference (replace 130-cmd dump) | task | EPIC-A | W5 |
| B17 | Surface ddx sync (FEAT-023) and dashboard (FEAT-020/021) on site | task | EPIC-A | W5 |
| B18 | Maturity honesty pass — re-badge quorum, plugin API, server UI | task | EPIC-A | W5 |
| B19 | Drop Dun from site copy | task | EPIC-A | W5 |
| B20 | De-emphasize quorum review on features page | task | EPIC-A | W5 |
| B21 | Build site, verify links, run ddx doc audit | task | B5, B7, B11, B13, B14, B16, B17 | W6 |
| B22 | Update FEAT-003 (website) to reflect new IA | task | B21 | W6 |

## Acceptance criteria by bead (high-level)

**B1**: FEAT-005 includes `research` and `reference` artifact kinds with documented frontmatter schemas and ID prefixes.

**B2**: All files previously in `docs/resources/` are present under `docs/helix/00-discover/references/` with `REF-NNN` IDs and normalized frontmatter; `docs/resources/` contains only a stub README.

**B3**: At least 12 new `REF-NNN` artifacts exist for the sources enumerated in W2.3 (spec-kit, Kiro, MS factories, Lost in the Middle, Chroma, Sheridan & Verplank, OWASP, MAST, Helland, FrugalGPT, Self-Refine, Knuth).

**B4**: 10 `RSCH-NNN` artifacts exist (one per principle), each 300–500 words, each with `depends_on` listing relevant `REF-*` artifacts.

**B5**: `docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md` has the 10 domain principle headlines (per locked list above), each with paragraph + DDx response + `depends_on` to the right `RSCH-NNN`. Preface frames the list as software-factory design choices.

**B6**: 10 deep pages exist at `website/content/docs/principles/<slug>/index.md`. Each page renders the principle headline, expanded paragraph, evidence summary, and DDx feature mapping.

**B7**: `website/content/why/_index.md` has one section per principle (50–80 words) linking to the deep page. Old content replaced.

**B8**: `product-vision.md` has a "Lineage" section under the introduction citing software-factories, spec-driven, and agent-execution waves with links to REF-* artifacts.

2. **Quick start** — copy-pasteable installation command, 3-step getting started guide
3. **Concepts page** — explains document-driven development, document types, how DDx fits with agents and workflow tools

input.new_string:
### Functional

1. **Landing page** — hero section explaining DDx in one sentence, with the sub-claim **"A document-driven software factory."** Includes visual ecosystem diagram, call-to-action to install, and a link to the new `/docs/concepts/software-factory/` page.
2. **Quick start** — copy-pasteable installation command, 3-step getting started guide
3. **Concepts pages** — explain document-driven development, document types, how DDx fits with agents and workflow tools. Includes:
   - `/docs/concepts/software-factory.md` — DDx as a document-driven software factory (lineage, what is inherited, what is added, what is intentionally not done)
   - `/docs/concepts/run-architecture.md` — three-layer run model (`ddx run` / `ddx try` / `ddx work`); retires `execute-loop` / `execute-bead` from primary copy
   - Existing concept pages (architecture, document model, etc.) updated to use the new naming
4. **Why DDx (`/why`)** — top-level page rewritten as 10 sections, one per **domain principle**. Each section has a 50–80 word summary linking to a deep page at `/docs/principles/<slug>/`. The 10 domain principles (locked):
   1. Spec-first development
   2. Executable specifications
   3. Audit trail required
   4. Context is king
   5. Work is a DAG
   6. Right-size the model
   7. Avoid vendor lock-in
   8. Drift is debt
   9. Least privilege for agents
   10. Inspect and adapt
5. **Principle deep pages** — 10 pages at `/docs/principles/<slug>/index.md`, each rendered from the corresponding `RSCH-NNN` research artifact in `docs/helix/00-discover/research/`. Each deep page presents claim → evidence → DDx-feature mapping.
6. **CLI reference** — curated noun-verb command documentation matching the command overview in `CLAUDE.md`
7. **Ecosystem page** — shows DDx's position as infrastructure, lists workflow tools built on DDx, highlights HELIX as first plugin. **Dun is removed from all site copy** (no longer a peer in the ecosystem).
8. **Server documentation** — how to run ddx-server, MCP endpoint reference
9. **Embedded terminal demos** — asciinema recordings of core workflows embedded in hero section and getting-started page:

input.old_string:
- Install DDx
   - `ddx init` + `ddx list` + `ddx doctor`
   - `ddx install helix` (plugin bootstrap)
   - One-shot project creation with HELIX
   - Feature evolution with HELIX
8. **README** — animated GIF/SVG demos, badge row, plugin quick start, link to microsite. The README is the GitHub-facing landing page and must sell at a glance.
input.new_string:
- Install DDx
   - `ddx init` + `ddx list` + `ddx doctor`
   - `ddx install helix` (plugin bootstrap)
   - One-shot project creation with HELIX
   - Feature evolution with HELIX
10. **README** — animated GIF/SVG demos, badge row, plugin quick start, link to microsite. The README is the GitHub-facing landing page and must sell at a glance.

### Content stance

- **Dun removed.** No references to Dun anywhere in site copy, ecosystem diagrams, or glossary.
- **Quorum de-emphasized.** Multi-model review (quorum) is no longer a primary capability and is removed from the features list. It is documented only in skill-level pages and badged as "framing" (matches `FEAT-013` status).
- **Naming.** `ddx run` / `ddx try` / `ddx work` is the unified primary naming. `execute-loop` / `execute-bead` appear only inside the CLI reference, not in primary copy.
- **Software factory framing.** The homepage, `docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md` (new "Lineage" section), and `docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md` (new preface) all frame DDx as a document-driven software factory.

### Information architecture

```
/                                  → homepage (sub-claim: "A document-driven software factory.")
/why                               → 10 principle summaries (domain principles)
/docs/getting-started              → install + first steps
/docs/concepts/software-factory    → factory framing
/docs/concepts/run-architecture    → run/try/work three-layer model
/docs/concepts/<existing>          → architecture, document model, etc.
/docs/principles/<slug>/           → 10 deep pages, one per domain principle
/docs/cli/                         → curated noun-verb CLI reference
/docs/server/                      → ddx-server + MCP endpoint reference
/docs/ecosystem                    → DDx + HELIX positioning (Dun removed)
```

The `/why` summaries and `/docs/principles/<slug>/` deep pages are the user-facing **domain principle taxonomy** (10 pages). The complementary 6 **engineering principles** are internal and live in `docs/dev/engineering-principles.md`, not on the site.

input.new_string:
### Pages Built

- Landing page with hero, feature grid, CTAs, software-factory sub-claim
- Getting Started (install, init, first steps)
- `/why` — 10 domain principle summaries with links to deep pages
- `/docs/principles/<slug>/` — 10 deep pages backed by `RSCH-NNN` research artifacts
- `/docs/concepts/software-factory.md` — software-factory framing
- `/docs/concepts/run-architecture.md` — `ddx run` / `ddx try` / `ddx work` three-layer model
- Other concepts pages (document-driven development explanation, document model, architecture)
- CLI Reference (curated noun-verb reference)
- DDx Server (MCP endpoints, HTTP API — marked as under development)
- Ecosystem (stack diagram, what-goes-where table; Dun removed)
